Title: Transcriptional regulation of plant secondary metabolism

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mediates nicotine translocation in aerial parts and deposition in vacuoles
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Nicotine is biosynthesized in roots but accumulates mainly in leaf vacuoles. 2. Nt-JAT1 is responsible for nicotine translocation in aerial parts. 3. It also mediates vacuolar deposition of nicotine.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included in the review as a non-TF example of transport-dependent metabolite accumulation.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Recruits MYB and bHLH factors to form anthocyanin activation complex
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GL3; EGL3; TT8; MYB75; MYB90; MYB113; MYB114
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TTG1 is the WD-repeat component of the anthocyanin regulatory complex. 2. It recruits bHLH and MYB proteins. 3. The complex upregulates anthocyanin biosynthetic genes.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a TF or pathway enzyme; treated as common gene due to scaffold-like regulatory role.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances differentiation of non-colored plastids into chromoplasts for carotenoid accumulation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OR promotes chromoplast differentiation. 2. This enhances carotenoid accumulation in cauliflower. 3. It exemplifies non-transcriptional regulation of secondary metabolite accumulation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned in Perspectives as an alternative regulatory mechanism outside TF control.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Inverted clusters of three CHS genes cause silencing of all CHS genes and dominant inhibition of seed coat pigmentation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. In soybean, an inverted cluster of three CHS genes triggers silencing. 2. This silences all CHS genes. 3. The result is dominant inhibition of seed coat pigmentation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned as an RNA-silencing-based mechanism rather than TF-mediated regulation.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms upstream committed precursor for target product
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly active in glandular trichomes on leaves, stems, and inflorescences
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly active in glandular trichomes on leaves, stems, and inflorescences
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Glandular trichomes on leaves, stems, and inflorescences
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports artemisinin biosynthesis and its tissue-specific accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ADS is a core artemisinin pathway gene summarized in the review. 2. Its expression is highly active in glandular trichomes where artemisinin accumulates. 3. It is activated by AaERF1, AaERF2, and AaWRKY1 according to cited studies.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a representative downstream target of artemisinin-regulating TFs in this review.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidation steps downstream of amorpha-4,11-diene in artemisinin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate pathway intermediates toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly active in glandular trichomes on leaves, stems, and inflorescences
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly active in glandular trichomes on leaves, stems, and inflorescences
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Glandular trichomes on leaves, stems, and inflorescences
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports artemisinin and artemisininic acid accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP71AV1 is a core artemisinin pathway gene summarized in the review. 2. It is highly expressed in glandular trichomes. 3. AaERF1 and AaERF2 activate its promoter according to cited work.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific oxidation sequence not detailed in this review.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces a double bond in an artemisinin-pathway intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly active in glandular trichomes on leaves, stems, and inflorescences
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly active in glandular trichomes on leaves, stems, and inflorescences
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Glandular trichomes on leaves, stems, and inflorescences
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with artemisinin biosynthesis in glandular trichomes
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DBR2 is listed as an artemisinin biosynthetic gene. 2. Its expression is highly active in glandular trichomes where artemisinin accumulates. 3. It is part of the summarized pathway rather than a direct TF target detailed here.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not directly functionally dissected in this review.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes an aldehyde intermediate in the artemisinin pathway
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly active in glandular trichomes on leaves, stems, and inflorescences
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly active in glandular trichomes on leaves, stems, and inflorescences
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Glandular trichomes on leaves, stems, and inflorescences
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with artemisinin pathway flux in glandular trichomes
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ALDH1 is included among artemisinin biosynthetic genes with glandular trichome-enriched expression. 2. Its expression pattern matches artemisinin accumulation sites. 3. It is summarized as part of the pathway context for TF regulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct regulatory experiment on ALDH1 is detailed in this review.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the first committed step of gossypol biosynthesis to produce (+)-δ-cadinene
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms committed precursor to target product
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Undetectable until 20 d post-anthesis during embryo development; induced by fungal elicitor
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Undetectable until 20 d post-anthesis during embryo development; induced by fungal elicitor
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Pigmented glands of aerial organs; epidermal and subepidermal layers of roots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes gossypol biosynthesis in tissues with pigmented glands
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. cad1-C encodes the first committed enzyme of gossypol biosynthesis. 2. Its expression coincides with gland appearance during seed development. 3. It is induced by fungal elicitor treatment and is targeted by GaWRKY1.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Exact species/cultivar for cad1-C not specified in the review.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the second step of gossypol biosynthesis downstream of (+)-δ-cadinene
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms early intermediate toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Undetectable until 20 d post-anthesis during embryo development; induced by fungal elicitor
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Undetectable until 20 d post-anthesis during embryo development; induced by fungal elicitor
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Pigmented glands of aerial organs; epidermal and subepidermal layers of roots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports gossypol formation after cadinene synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP706B1 catalyzes the second step of gossypol biosynthesis. 2. Its expression appears during embryo development when glands form. 3. It is induced along with gossypol accumulation by fungal elicitation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not directly characterized as a TF target in detail here.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for target products
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: JA-inducible; promoter responsive to elicitor and JA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: JA-inducible; promoter responsive to elicitor and JA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports TIA biosynthesis by supplying tryptamine precursor
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TDC produces tryptamine for TIA biosynthesis. 2. Its promoter is regulated by CrWRKY1 and repressed by ZCT proteins. 3. It is part of the JA-responsive TIA regulatory network.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The review discusses TDC mainly as a direct transcriptional target.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses tryptamine and secologanin to form strictosidine
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms central precursor to monomeric and dimeric TIAs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of TIA precursors and downstream alkaloids
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. STR forms strictosidine, the central precursor of TIAs. 2. ORCA3 directly activates STR through the JERE promoter element. 3. ZCT proteins repress STR promoter activity.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also written as Str in parts of the review.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies shared precursor pool for indolic glucosinolates and camalexin
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP79B2 is a shared upstream enzyme for indolic glucosinolates and camalexin. 2. It is activated by AtMYB34/ATR1 in glucosinolate regulation. 3. It contributes to shared Trp-derived secondary metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also shared with auxin biosynthesis according to the review.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies shared precursor pool for indolic glucosinolates and camalexin
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP79B3 is a shared upstream enzyme for indolic glucosinolates and camalexin. 2. It is activated by AtMYB34/ATR1 in glucosinolate regulation. 3. It contributes to Trp-derived specialized metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed together with CYP79B2 in the review.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes downstream conversion of indole-3-acetaldoxime in glucosinolate biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Commits shared precursor toward glucosinolate branch
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP83B1 is induced by OBP2 and AtMYB34/ATR1. 2. It functions downstream of IAOx in glucosinolate biosynthesis. 3. Its expression level correlates with indolic glucosinolate accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also a key branch control point separating glucosinolate from other Trp-derived pathways.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts indole-3-acetaldoxime toward indole-3-acetonitrile in camalexin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directs shared precursor into camalexin branch
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports camalexin biosynthesis under pathogen/elicitor conditions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP71A12 participates in conversion of IAOx toward camalexin. 2. Its expression is reduced in anac042 mutants. 3. It is part of the pathogen-induced camalexin pathway summarized in the review.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as one of the genes reduced in anac042 mutants.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts indole-3-acetaldoxime to indole-3-acetonitrile in camalexin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directs shared precursor into camalexin branch
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP71A13 is a camalexin pathway enzyme downstream of IAOx. 2. Its expression is reduced in anac042 mutants. 3. It contributes to pathogen-inducible camalexin formation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned with CYP71A12 as parallel enzymes in IAOx conversion.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the final conversion to camalexin via dihydrocamalexic acid
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pseudomonas syringae; Botrytis cinerea; Alternaria brassicicola infection
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP71B15/PAD3 catalyzes the final conversion to camalexin. 2. WRKY33 binds its promoter in response to pathogen infection. 3. Its expression is also reduced in anac042 mutants.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Central direct target in camalexin transcriptional regulation.

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Anthocyanin biosynthetic genes including DFR; anthocyanin synthase; glucosyltransferase
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates anthocyanin pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex with TTG1 and GL3/TT8/EGL3
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive anthocyanin activator in vegetative tissues
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtMYB75 is a representative MYB activator of anthocyanin biosynthesis. 2. It acts in the MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex. 3. It upregulates anthocyanin structural genes and increases anthocyanin accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also called PAP1 in broader literature, but not named here.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates the phenylpropanoid/anthocyanin pathway
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex with TTG1 and GL3/TT8/EGL3
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive anthocyanin activator
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtMYB90 is a representative activator of anthocyanin accumulation. 2. It functions with bHLH and WD40 proteins in a transcriptional complex. 3. It activates anthocyanin biosynthetic gene expression.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates anthocyanin biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex with TTG1 and GL3/TT8/EGL3
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive anthocyanin activator
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtMYB113 is a representative MYB regulator of anthocyanin biosynthesis. 2. It acts with TTG1 and bHLH partners. 3. It contributes to transcriptional activation of anthocyanin genes.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates anthocyanin biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex with TTG1 and GL3/TT8/EGL3
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive anthocyanin activator
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtMYB114 is a representative MYB regulator of anthocyanin biosynthesis. 2. It participates in the MYB-bHLH-WD40 activation complex. 3. It increases anthocyanin pathway gene expression.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates BAN expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Seed coat proanthocyanidin regulator
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes proanthocyanidin accumulation in seed coat
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TT2 regulates seed coat proanthocyanidins. 2. It controls BAN expression. 3. It is a representative R2R3-MYB regulator of flavonoid branch specificity.

Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive regulator of aliphatic glucosinolate biosynthesis in aerial tissues
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtMYB29 regulates aliphatic glucosinolate biosynthesis. 2. Its action is prominent in aerial tissues. 3. It is summarized as a representative MYB regulator in this pathway.

Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive regulator of aliphatic glucosinolate biosynthesis in aerial tissues
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtMYB76 regulates aliphatic glucosinolate biosynthesis. 2. It functions in aerial tissues. 3. It is grouped with AtMYB29 as a positive regulator.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates CYP79B2, CYP79B3, and CYP83B1 expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Controls indolic glucosinolate production via Trp-metabolism genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases indolyl glucosinolate and IAA accumulation but not aliphatic glucosinolates
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Dominant overexpression allele; recessive mutant; gene expression and metabolite analysis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtMYB34/ATR1 controls indolic glucosinolate production. 2. Its dominant overexpression increases CYP79B2, CYP79B3, and CYP83B1 expression and indolyl glucosinolates. 3. The recessive mutant shows reduced expression and metabolite accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified in a screen for altered tryptophan metabolism.

Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Controls indolic glucosinolate production
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtMYB51 is a representative regulator of indolic glucosinolate biosynthesis. 2. It functions in roots and late-stage rosette leaves. 3. It regulates Trp-metabolizing P450 genes according to the review.

Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Controls indolic glucosinolate production
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtMYB122 is a representative regulator of indolic glucosinolate biosynthesis. 2. It acts in roots and late-stage rosette leaves. 3. It is grouped with AtMYB34 and AtMYB51 in control of Trp-metabolizing P450 genes.

Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Representative tobacco MYB involved in phenylpropanoid regulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NtMYBJS1 is involved in phenylpropanoid regulation in tobacco. 2. It is cited as a representative MYB regulator. 3. Specific downstream targets are not detailed in this review.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Correlates with anthocyanin spots in tepals and light-induced pigmentation in leaves
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Determines tissue-specific anthocyanin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LhMYB6 is a positive regulator of anthocyanin biosynthesis in lily. 2. Its expression correlates with anthocyanin spots in tepals. 3. It is associated with light-induced leaf pigmentation.

Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Determines organ- and tissue-specific anthocyanin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin pigmentation in specific floral organs
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LhMYB12 is a positive regulator of lily anthocyanin biosynthesis. 2. Tissue-specific expression determines pigmentation in tepals, filaments, and styles. 3. It helps specify organ-specific anthocyanin accumulation.

Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Insertion of a Copia-like retrotransposon adjacent to Ruby results in cold-dependent anthocyanin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Confers cold-dependent anthocyanin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates anthocyanin accumulation in blood oranges under cold conditions
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ruby is a MYB transcription activator associated with anthocyanin accumulation in blood oranges. 2. A nearby retrotransposon insertion confers cold-dependent regulation. 3. It exemplifies environment-dependent transcriptional activation of pigmentation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Table in the review incorrectly lists species as Camellia sinensis; text clearly refers to blood oranges.

Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Determines anthocyanin accumulation in fruit flesh
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation in apple flesh
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB10 determines anthocyanin accumulation in apple fruit flesh. 2. Its action is tissue-specific. 3. It contrasts with MYB1/MYBA controlling fruit skin pigmentation.

Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Determines anthocyanin accumulation in fruit skin
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation in apple skin
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB1/MYBA controls anthocyanin accumulation in apple fruit skin. 2. Its action is skin-specific. 3. It is distinct from flesh regulator MYB10 despite high sequence identity.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Seasonal expression in skin and seeds responsive to light conditions
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Correlated with anthocyanin accumulation in skin and seeds
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvMYBPA1 expression correlates with anthocyanin accumulation in grape skin and seeds. 2. Its expression responds seasonally to light. 3. It is a representative grape MYB regulator in the review.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Seasonal expression in skin and seeds responsive to light conditions
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvMYB5a expression correlates with anthocyanin accumulation in grape tissues. 2. It responds to light conditions. 3. It is summarized as part of the seasonal MYB regulatory set.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Seasonal expression in skin and seeds responsive to light conditions
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvMYB5b expression correlates with anthocyanin accumulation in grape tissues. 2. It responds to seasonal light conditions. 3. It is included as a representative grape MYB regulator.

Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Core bHLH partner in anthocyanin activation complex
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GL3 is a bHLH component of the anthocyanin activation complex. 2. It works with TTG1 and MYB factors. 3. The complex activates anthocyanin structural genes.

Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Core bHLH partner in anthocyanin activation complex
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EGL3 is a bHLH component of the anthocyanin activation complex. 2. It partners with TTG1 and MYB proteins. 3. It contributes to activation of anthocyanin biosynthetic genes.

Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Core bHLH partner in anthocyanin activation complex
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TT8 is a bHLH regulator in the anthocyanin complex. 2. It interacts with MYB proteins and TTG1. 3. It activates anthocyanin structural gene expression.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates ORCA3 expression by binding the JA-responsive element in its promoter
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Connects JA signaling to a subset of TIA biosynthesis genes
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrMYC2 directly binds the ORCA3 promoter. 2. It activates ORCA3 and thereby regulates a subset of TIA biosynthesis genes. 3. It links JA signaling to alkaloid accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter binding and expression activation reported in cited study

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Upregulates sesquiterpene synthase gene expression in flowers
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with DELLA and JAZ signaling regulators
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Integrates JA and GA signaling inputs
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key integrator of JA and GA control of floral sesquiterpenes
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Arabidopsis MYC2 upregulates sesquiterpene synthase genes in flowers. 2. It integrates jasmonate and gibberellin signaling. 3. It interacts with DELLA and JAZ proteins in this regulatory module.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Upregulates ORCA-related NIC2 locus genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Enhances nicotine biosynthesis through NIC2 AP2/ERF regulators
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NtMYC2a/b enhance nicotine biosynthesis. 2. They act by upregulating the ORCA-related NIC2 locus. 3. They exemplify bHLH-to-AP2/ERF hierarchical regulation.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Binds G-box elements in the putrescine N-methyltransferase promoter and activates expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive regulator correlated with nicotine accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: EST screening and virus-induced gene silencing
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbbHLH1 is correlated with nicotine accumulation. 2. It binds G-box elements in the putrescine N-methyltransferase promoter. 3. It acts as a positive regulator of nicotine biosynthesis.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Binds G-box elements in the putrescine N-methyltransferase promoter and activates expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive regulator correlated with nicotine accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: EST screening and virus-induced gene silencing
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbbHLH2 is correlated with nicotine accumulation. 2. It binds G-box motifs in the putrescine N-methyltransferase promoter. 3. It acts as a positive regulator of nicotine biosynthesis.

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: STR and other TIA biosynthetic genes via JERE elements
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates TIA pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: JA-dependent AP2/ERF activator of TIA pathway
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ORCA2 is a representative AP2/ERF regulator of TIAs. 2. It binds JERE elements in TIA gene promoters. 3. It participates in JA-dependent induction of alkaloid biosynthesis.

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: STR; multiple genes in plastidial IPP pathway and secondary TIA pathway
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates STR by direct binding to JERE and upregulates multiple TIA biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major AP2/ERF activator linking JA signaling to multiple TIA genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression promotes TIA accumulation in suspension cells
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Direct promoter binding; overexpression in suspension cells
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ORCA3 directly activates STR through the JERE promoter element. 2. It controls both primary plastidial IPP and secondary TIA pathway genes. 3. Its overexpression increases TIA accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts downstream of CrMYC2 in the summarized hierarchy.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Binds promoter motifs in ADS and CYP71AV1 and activates both genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: JA-responsive activator of key artemisinin pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases artemisinin and artemisininic acids
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AaERF1 is JA responsive. 2. It binds ADS and CYP71AV1 promoters and activates both genes. 3. Overexpression increases artemisinin and artemisininic acids.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Binds promoter motifs in ADS and CYP71AV1 and activates both genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: JA-responsive activator of key artemisinin pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases artemisinin and artemisininic acids
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AaERF2 is JA responsive. 2. It binds ADS and CYP71AV1 promoters and activates these genes. 3. Overexpression increases artemisinin and artemisininic acids.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Binds cadinene synthase promoter and activates expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive regulator of gossypol biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GaWRKY1 is a positive regulator of gossypol biosynthesis. 2. It binds the promoter of a (+)-δ-cadinene synthase gene. 3. It connects stress-responsive WRKY signaling with cotton phytoalexin production.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Binds and activates sesquiterpene synthase gene promoter
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive regulator of artemisinin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AaWRKY1 is a positive regulator of artemisinin biosynthesis. 2. It binds and activates a sesquiterpene synthase promoter. 3. It exemplifies WRKY control of defensive terpenoid metabolism.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Binds CYP71B15/PAD3 promoter and stimulates expression
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Pseudomonas syringae infection; Botrytis cinerea infection; MAP kinase cascades
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Released from MKS1/WRKY33 complex upon MKS1 phosphorylation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Central pathogen-responsive activator of camalexin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes camalexin accumulation and pathogen resistance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter binding; mutant susceptibility; overexpression resistance; phosphorylation studies
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. WRKY33 binds the PAD3 promoter in response to pathogen infection. 2. wrky33 mutants are susceptible to fungal pathogens, whereas overexpression increases resistance. 3. MAP kinase cascades activate WRKY33-dependent camalexin biosynthesis.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation with broader network rewiring
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Binds W-box in TDC promoter and activates expression; overexpression upregulates several key pathway genes and ZCT repressors but represses ORCA2, ORCA3, and CrMYC2
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Determines root-specific accumulation of serpentine
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases serpentine about threefold but decreases catharanthine and tabersonine
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter binding; overexpression in hairy roots; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrWRKY1 directly activates TDC. 2. Overexpression remodels the TIA regulatory network, increasing ZCTs and repressing ORCA2/3 and CrMYC2. 3. It shifts alkaloid output toward serpentine in roots.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Illustrates metabolite branch redistribution rather than uniform TIA increase.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses TDC and STR promoter activity; represses ORCA activating activity on STR promoter
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Negative regulator limiting TIA pathway gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast one-hybrid screening; sequence-specific promoter binding; promoter repression assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZCT1 binds TDC and STR promoters. 2. It represses their activity. 3. It also antagonizes ORCA-mediated activation of STR.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses TDC and STR promoter activity; represses ORCA activating activity on STR promoter
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Negative regulator limiting TIA pathway gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast one-hybrid screening; sequence-specific promoter binding; promoter repression assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZCT2 binds TDC and STR promoters. 2. It represses promoter activity. 3. It antagonizes ORCA-mediated activation of STR.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses TDC and STR promoter activity; represses ORCA activating activity on STR promoter
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Negative regulator limiting TIA pathway gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast one-hybrid screening; sequence-specific promoter binding; promoter repression assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZCT3 binds TDC and STR promoters. 2. It represses promoter activity. 3. It suppresses ORCA activation on STR promoter.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Overexpression activates CYP83B1; silencing suppresses CYP83B1 expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Part of the Arabidopsis glucosinolate regulatory network
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OBP2 is responsive to herbivores, pathogens, and JA. 2. It positively regulates CYP83B1 expression. 3. It functions in the glucosinolate regulatory network.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Required for normal expression of these camalexin P450 genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: NAC regulator required for full camalexin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mutation reduces camalexin accumulation and increases susceptibility to Alternaria brassicicola
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ANAC042 is required for full pathogen-induced camalexin accumulation. 2. anac042 mutants show reduced expression of CYP71A12, CYP71A13, and PAD3. 3. It may function independently of WRKY33-dependent signaling.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: First NAC TF implicated in plant secondary metabolism regulation according to the review.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression via interference with activation complex
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: DFR through interference with MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Reduces DFR expression by interfering with the MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Negative age-dependent regulator of anthocyanin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SPL9 negatively regulates anthocyanin accumulation. 2. It interferes with the MYB-bHLH-WD40 activation complex. 3. miR156 and SPL9 mediate age-dependent anthocyanin regulation.
